g.text: Understanding the Five V's and Beyond

g.data-narration: Big Data challenges revolve around five key dimensions known as the Five V's: Volume, Velocity, Variety, Veracity, and Value.
g.text: The Five V's of Big Data

g.data-narration: Every day, approximately 2.5 quintillion bytes of data are generated worldwide, doubling every two years.
g.text: • 90% of world's data in last 2 years

g.data-narration: Volume challenges involve storing petabytes/exabytes of data, requiring distributed storage like HDFS and horizontal scaling.
Velocity challenges require real-time processing using stream frameworks like Kafka, Flink, and Spark Streaming.
Variety challenges involve integrating structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data with different formats.
Veracity addresses data quality issues: accuracy, completeness, consistency, and trustworthiness of data.
Value challenges involve extracting actionable insights from data using ML/AI while measuring ROI of data initiatives.
g.text: Many organizations fail to extract value

g.data-narration: Security challenges include encryption, access control, and regulatory compliance like GDPR across distributed environments.
Talent scarcity in Big Data skills remains a major bottleneck with high demand for distributed computing and ML experts.
CAP Theorem states distributed systems can only guarantee two of three: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance.
g.text: Choose 2 of 3: CA, CP, or AP based on requirements

g.data-narration: Lambda architecture combines batch and stream processing; Kappa architecture uses stream-only for simpler systems.
g.text: Lambda: complex but accurate | Kappa: simple but reprocesses streams

g.data-narration: Social media analytics example: processes billions of posts using Kafka and Spark, handles variety with Lambda architecture.
Healthcare example: protects EHR with encryption, RBAC, and differential privacy while enabling research analysis.
IoT example: edge computing reduces data volume, sub-second latency with Flink, tiered storage with lifecycle policies.
Technology mapping: HDFS/Cloud for volume, Kafka/Stream for velocity, JSON/Avro/Parquet for variety challenges.
Exam tips: memorize five Vs, understand technology mappings, know CAP theorem trade-offs, and schema evolution strategies.
